stem: In triangle PQR, angle R is a right angle and the length of segment QR is 16 units. If tan P = 8/15, what is the perimeter, in units, of triangle PQR?
rationale: In right triangle PQR with right angle at R, tan P = (opposite)/(adjacent) = QR/PR = 8/15. Given QR = 16, we have 16/PR = 8/15, so PR = 30. By the Pythagorean theorem, PQ = √(16² + 30²) = √(256 + 900) = √1156 = 34. Perimeter = 16 + 30 + 34 = 80. Choice A is just the hypotenuse; B is just PR; C is QR + PR.
